title: Capaciteitsmanager Business Clients & Ecosystems
description:
In het kort
Ben jij die ervaren Capaciteitsmanager die workforce management binnen onze afdeling naar een hoger niveau wil brengen? Ben je een ervaren WFM-er, slim, ambitieus, analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig?  Weet jij als geen ander hoe je interne stakeholders moet overtuigen van jouw adviezen en bevindingen? Dan gaan wij graag met jou in gesprek!
Je werk
Je werkt in een klein WFM team en bent daar verantwoordelijk voor de WFM cyclus en het forecastproces bij Business Clients. Je maakt de forecast voor alle werkstromen die met name via Microsoft Dynamics lopen en onderzoekt hoe we in de toekomst alle werkzaamheden van de business kunnen forcecasten en inplannen via WFM. Je stelt een capaciteitsplan op en geeft het management aan de hand hiervan advies over benodigde capaciteit en werkverdeling. De uitkomsten van het capaciteitsoverleg draag je over aan de Planners en Traffic managers. Je levert een proactieve bijdrage aan de inzet en optimalisatie van WFM. Je rapporteert aan de manager WFM BC&E. Het betreft een fulltime functie.
Werkomgeving
Bij het Centre of Expertise (CoE) WFM zijn alle WFM experts binnen de bank ondergebracht die voor verschillende afdelingen WFM neerzetten en uitvoeren. Je komt te werken in het executie team dat verantwoordelijk is voor WFM bij Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E).
Je standplaats wordt de Wisselwerking 58 in Diemen maar bij het Centre of Expertise WFM werken wij hybride, hierbij bepalen de werkzaamheden wat voor jou die dag de beste werkplek is.
Business Clients & Ecosystems
De client unit Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E) is verantwoordelijk voor het bedienen van zakelijke klanten met een omzet tot 25 miljoen Euro, ingedeeld in 'sectorclusters'. De focus van BC&E ligt op het behalen van onze groeidoelstelling om de nummer één bank voor het midden- en kleinbedrijf  (zakelijke klanten met omzet tot 25 miljoen) te worden.
BC&E bedient +/- 370.000 zakelijke klanten (inclusief Medical Business Clients). De afdeling adviseert zakelijke klanten over dagelijkse zakelijke producten en diensten, zoals leningen tot een miljoen Euro, betaaloplossingen, sparen, verzekeringen, beyond en near banking proposities en ondersteuning bij het dagelijks bankieren.
Je profiel
Meerdere jaren werkervaring op het gebied van Workforce management (WFM); Ervaring met het implementeren van WFM bij een nieuwe afdeling is een pre.
Analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig.
Kennis en ervaring met PowerBI rapportages is een pre.
Wij bieden
Het is voor ons belangrijk om een goede werkgever te zijn. Wij bieden je daarom een functie met impact én uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden die bijdragen aan jouw werkplezier, ontwikkeling en wellbeing.
Daarnaast bieden wij:
Een aantrekkelijk bruto maandsalaris op basis van een 36urige werkweek. Dit is inclusief vakantiegeld en een flexibel benefit budget.
Een uitstekende pensioenregeling, zodat je goed voorbereid bent voor later.
Flexibiliteit in werken: thuiswerken is mogelijk in overleg met je team en afhankelijk van je functie. Wij regelen een ergonomische thuiswerkplek voor je.
Ruimte voor ontspanning met vijf weken vakantie per jaar, aangevuld met twee verplichte vrije dagen. Daarnaast kun je jaarlijks tot vier extra weken vakantie bijkopen.
Vijf ‘Banking for better days’: extra vrije dagen die je kunt inzetten voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling of vrijwilligerswerk.
Persoonlijke ontwikkeling staat centraal: je ontvangt een ontwikkelbudget van €1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot €3.000.
Een OV-jaarabonnement met gratis openbaar vervoer in heel Nederland voor zakelijk en privé gebruik.
Interesse?
Heb je interesse? Reageer dan door middel van een sollicitatie. Voor extra inhoudelijke info graag even reageren naar
thomas.van.hassel@nl.abnamro.com
/ 06 1439 7379.
Jouw Toekomst: Inclusief, Innovatief en Duurzaam
Bij ABN AMRO geloven we in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Gelijke kansen voor iedereen vormen een belangrijk fundament, waarbij we streven naar een inclusieve cultuur waarin alle medewerkers zich gezien, gehoord en gewaardeerd voelen. Onze visie als
persoonlijke bank in de digitale tijd
sluit naadloos aan bij de behoefte aan verrassende inzichten en innovatieve oplossingen, voortkomend uit een divers samenspel van culturen en ervaringen. We richten ons op klantbeleving, duurzaamheid en bouwen aan een toekomstbestendige bank, waarbij we gelijke beloning voor gelijk werk jaarlijks onderzoeken om eerlijke kansen te waarborgen. Sluit je aan bij een bank die vindingrijkheid en ambitie omarmt, en maak samen met ons impact voor een betere toekomst.
Als je wilt solliciteren voor het B-Able of Reboot programma, zorg er dan voor dat je dit vermeldt zowel in de titel als in de inhoud van je cv.

Externe recruitmentbureaus dienen een overeenkomst met ABN AMRO BANK N.V. te hebben getekend, uitgegeven door een Talent Acquisition Specialist, om CV's te mogen indienen. Alle ongevraagde CV's die buiten deze voorwaarden worden aangeboden zullen als eigendom van ABN AMRO BANK N.V. worden beschouwd. ABN AMRO BANK N.V. is hierbij geen plaatsingskosten verschuldigd.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
At a glance
Are you passionate about both advanced analytics and empowering others to grow in their analytical capabilities? Do you have the expertise to lead by example while fostering a culture of statistical thinking? If so, we have the perfect role for you!
Channel Security is seeking a Lead Data Analyst to join our team; Fraud Monitoring. In this role, you will not only be a technical expert and a strong individual contributor but also a key leader in advancing the analytics maturity of a team with a business-driven background. You will serve as a pillar for analytics standards and help shape the way we use data to make impactful decisions.
Your job
As a Lead Data Analyst within the Fraud Monitoring team, you will play a central role in ensuring that our analysis and detection logic are grounded in solid reasoning and supported by evidence. You will work closely with Fraud Experts (FEs), Data Scientists, and Machine Learning Engineers to strengthen our fraud detection capabilities across payment channels to catch frauds such as phishing, account takeover, lost or stolen cards, and other emerging threats.
Your work focuses on deep‑dive analysis, understanding fraud behaviour, evaluating the effectiveness of detection logic, and validating whether proposed changes are analytically sound. You will help the team distinguish clear signals from noise, recognise when evidence is insufficient, and ensure our decisions are based on robust data rather than anecdotal cases.
This is a hands‑on analytical role with significant influence: your insights and your methodological judgment will directly shape how we detect fraud and protect our customers.
Key Responsibilities:
Analytical Excellence
Perform in‑depth analyses to understand fraud patterns, attack vectors, seasonality, behavioural shifts, and detection performance across payment channels.
Evaluate the strength of proposed detection logic (features, thresholds, rules) and identify when ideas are overly narrow, unsupported by evidence, or prone to false positives.
Assess the value and reliability of data fields and signals used in fraud detection.
Help ensure our reasoning, conclusions, and decisions are analytically sound and well‑supported.
Methodological Leadership
Guide Fraud Experts and junior analysts in applying good analytical practices and critical thinking.
Coach colleagues on interpreting data responsibly, avoiding common analytical pitfalls, and understanding basic statistical concepts such as variance, sample bias, and signal strength.
Promote a culture of analytical rigor — ensuring the team adopts more mature, data‑driven ways of working.
Collaboration & Impact
Partner with Data Scientists to transform analytical insights into hypotheses, experiments, and feature ideas for model development.
Act as a bridge between domain experts and technical experts, ensuring clear understanding on both sides.
Identify opportunities to improve how we measure fraud impact, effectiveness of controls, and operational efficiency.
Drive improvements in how we use data in the team — from investigation workflows to decision support tools — focusing on quality, clarity, and insight rather than dashboard volume.
Working environment
Grid Channel Security within Corporate Information Security Office (CISO) is responsible for the prevention and detection of payment fraud, and deciding the policy and strategy in the area of fraud. The Fraud Monitoring team that you are going to be part of, is a motivated multidisciplinary group of approximately 25 colleagues working with a focus on protecting customers. We work in a hybrid way and our day to day work is organized by the agile and scrum principles. CISO is recognised as having an informal work atmosphere where everyone has a heart for their job and is prepared to help others.
Your profile
Qualifications and technical skills:
A master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or similar).
At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role involving hands-on data exploration, problem solving, and interpretation (e.g., data analyst, fraud analyst, risk analyst, business analyst with strong technical skills, data scientists in analytical track).
Technical skills:
Strong SQL skills for exploratory analysis and investigation.
Hands-on experience in Python for data analysis.
PySpark or experience working with large datasets.
Basic familiarity with visualization tools for communication — not for dashboard engineering.
Ability to frame analytical questions, interpret results correctly, and identify flawed logic or insufficient evidence.
Familiarity with basic statistical concepts (e.g., distributions, variance, seasonality, sampling, simple backtesting principles). Not as a statistician— but as a critical thinker.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
About you
You are an energetic Firestarter, proactively looking for and finding relevant data analysis questions
Strong critical thinking and the confidence to challenge weak assumptions.
Ability to clearly articulate reasoning, uncertainties, and trade-offs.
A Teacher and Mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration
We are offering
Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work-life balance;
An industry-competitive salary based on a 36 or 40-hour work week;
A supplementary benefit budget of 11%, which you can spend on pre-tax fringe benefits;
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year;
An annual public transportation pass;
A solid pension plan.
Interested?
Does this opportunity sound like something you’d be interested in? Please send us your application as soon as possible. We look forward to meeting you.
Please note: This vacancy is a repost of a position previously published on 27 November 2025; if you have already received a rejection for this role, reapplying will not be considered.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.
If you want to apply for the B-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.
Disclaimer external recruitment agencies
External recruitment agencies need to have a signed agreement with ABN AMRO BANK N.V., executed by a Talent Acquisition Specialist, when submitting a resume to a vacancy. No unsolicited services or offers, please.

match.llm_rationale: The resume shows strong technical AI/ML skills but lacks direct experience in fraud analysis, financial domains, or data analyst roles, with minimal overlap in key responsibilities like fraud pattern analysis, detection logic evaluation, and methodological leadership required for this senior data analyst position.

title: Data & AI Strategy Advisor
description:
In het kort
Do you want to lead the data & AI revolution? Do you have a strong passion for data & AI and believe that these are at the core of unlocking value for customers? Do you get energised by the state of the art in a complex environment? Then come and help us unlock the power of data & AI at the Central Data Office (CDO) of ABN AMRO!
Je werk
You directly support the execution of the ABN AMRO strategy: a Personal Bank in a Digital Age. You are part of the Data & AI Strategy & Design team within the Central Data Office. You set the strategic direction, shape new concepts, and drive complex, bank‑wide initiatives at the intersection of data, AI, regulation and business strategy. You have a diverse stakeholder field, spanning across technical and business areas as well leadership and operational levels.
Key responsibilities include:
Maintain and further develop the bank‑wide AI Strategy.
Shape strategic direction and state of the art on topics such as:
AI‑ready data (data quality, metadata, lineage, governance‑by‑design)
AI technologies, with a strong focus on its potential and responsible usage.
Define operating models, roles and responsibilities to translate the strategy into execution.
Spot and assess external trends and internal drivers for change, including developments in AI, data regulation and technology.
Act as a trusted sparring partner for the Head of Data & AI Governance, the Chief Data Officer and senior leadership.
Lead and support transformational initiatives (e.g. AI Strategy execution).
Ensure strategic recommendations are translated into concrete actions, roadmaps and decisions, and actively followed through to implementation.
Prepare and support executive decision‑making for the Executive Board and Supervisory Board.
Werkomgeving
You will work within the Central Data Office – Data and AI Governance – Strategy & Design team, closely collaborating with other CDO teams, IT teams, data domains, business lines, international branches and subsidiaries. We are in constant collaboration with all business lines, international branches and subsidiaries of the bank. Together, we operate at the forefront of data and AI in banking, working with some of ABN AMRO’s most critical value assets.
We work in an open and transparent environment, where your opinion is valued and dialogue is encouraged. The team is diverse with regards to culture, experience and age. The atmosphere is lively and informal, closely monitoring a safe environment where everyone can share their thoughts, feelings and worries. It is normal to make mistakes, and we aim to help each other grow better as learning is one of our main focuses.
Je profiel
You are a senior, execution‑oriented strategiser. You thrive in ambiguity and bridges strategy and delivery, backed by a strong background in consulting, driving complex programmes, particularly on AI. You can quickly master new topics, form clear strategic opinions, and translate strategy into executable change within a complex organisation.
Experience & skills
Master’s degree.
Proven experience in AI, in addition to Analytics and Data Management.
Strong consulting background, with experience advising senior stakeholders.
Experience leading enterprise‑wide data or AI initiatives.
Familiar with frameworks and regulations such as AI Act, DAMA‑DMBOK, Data Mesh, BCBS239, or similar.
Strong analytical, conceptual and communication skills.
What truly distinguishes you:
You learn new topics quickly and form clear, balanced strategic opinions tailored to executive audiences.
You naturally connect external trends to the internal context of ABN AMRO.
You can navigate organisational complexity and tailor solutions to different stakeholders and maturity levels.
You go beyond advice proactively: you turn recommendations into action and ensure follow‑through to implementation.
Wij bieden
We believe it is important to be a good employer. Joining us means stepping into a role with impact, supported by excellent employment conditions that foster your job satisfaction, development, and wellbeing.
In addition, we offer:
An attractive gross monthly salary based on a 36hour work week, including holiday allowance and a flexible benefit budget.
An excellent pension scheme, ensuring that you are well prepared for the future.
Flexibility in working: working from home is possible in consultation with your team and depending on your role. We will provide an ergonomic home office setup for you.
Plenty of room for relaxation with five weeks of vacation per year, supplemented by two mandatory days off. You can also purchase up to four additional weeks of vacation annually.
Five “Banking for better days”: extra days off that you can use for personal development or volunteer work.
Personal development is key: you receive a development budget of €1,000 per year, which can accumulate up to €3,000.
An annual public transport pass with free public transportation throughout the Netherlands for both business and private use.
You can find more information about our employment conditions and salary scales in our Collective Labour Agreement (CLA), which applies to this vacancy.
Interesse?
Please apply!

title: Software Engineering Manager – Unified Platform
description:
This is Adyen
Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft — making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.
For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
Why you should join the Unified Platform
The
Unified Platform
powers the core financial infrastructure behind Adyen’s platform - from how transactions are processed to how financial data is stored, monitored, and configured. It plays a critical role in ensuring that payments, accounting, reporting, and reconciliation work seamlessly across all products and customer segments.
We are currently hiring
four Software Engineering Managers
within the Unified Platform. These teams work on different parts of the platform but share a common mission: building scalable, reliable systems that power Adyen’s global financial ecosystem.
Depending on your experience and strengths, you may join one of the following domains:
Transaction Platform
Build and support Adyen’s core transaction platform by providing a highly available API to store and retrieve transaction data and events across the system.
Storage & Scale
Design and evolve the storage architecture behind Adyen’s financial systems, ensuring our data infrastructure can scale reliably as transaction volumes grow.
Finance Observability
Enable transparent and reliable financial operations by building monitoring and alerting systems for critical financial flows across the platform.
Unified Configuration
Create a consistent platform experience by standardizing internal infrastructure and configuration systems that power Adyen’s financial products.
Your final team placement will be determined based on your skills, experience, and the technical fit identified during the interview process.
What you’ll do
Lead, grow, and mentor a team of 5–6 engineers building critical components of Adyen’s financial infrastructure.
Guide the design and development of scalable distributed systems using Core Java, web services, queueing frameworks, REST APIs, and SQL/PostgreSQL.
Collaborate closely with product and platform teams to design systems that are reliable, scalable, and easy to operate.
Balance long-term technical improvements with product delivery.
Foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
Set clear team goals aligned with engineering and product strategy.
Improve operational excellence through automation, observability, and system reliability.
Hire and develop engineering talent that thrives in a fast-moving environment.
Who you are
A strong people manager who enjoys coaching engineers and enabling teams to perform at their best.
Technically proficient in backend engineering, with strong experience in Java or other modern programming languages used to build large-scale distributed systems.
Comfortable making difficult decisions and communicating clearly with strong senior engineers.
Skilled at collaborating across teams and aligning stakeholders around technical decisions.
A clear communicator who can explain complex technical concepts to both engineers and non-technical audiences.
Experienced working with senior leadership to shape technical strategy and roadmaps.
Minimum qualifications
3+ years of people management experience
leading engineering teams.
8+ years of experience in backend software engineering.

title: Staff Engineer, Monetization
description:
This is Adyen
Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.
For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
As a
Staff Engineer for Monetization
, you will be the technical compass for how Adyen’s systems earn revenue at scale. Our monetization domain underpins how we price, bill, and account for all Adyen products, making it one of the most impactful areas of the company.
You will focus on the long-term evolution of our architecture: anticipating challenges, spotting opportunities, and ensuring we have the right technical foundations for the next generation of revenue systems. Rather than writing every line of code yourself, you will work closely with Tech Leads and Senior Engineers: coaching, guiding, and aligning them toward excellent, scalable solutions that keep our systems robust and reliable
You’ll have the space to think, innovate, and make high-leverage technical decisions that will shape how Adyen monetizes its products for years to come.
What you’ll do
Architect and drive adoption
of scalable and high-performance data pipelines and monetization framework components.
Continuously assess and improve existing systems
to enhance performance, reliability, and scalability
Evaluate and integrate new tools and technologies
to optimize our monetization ecosystem
Lead through influence
by building strong relationships across the organization
Proactively define and advocate best practices
in terms of performance, code quality, data validation, data governance, and discoverability
Guide and mentor
other engineers in advanced engineering and architectural principles, fostering a culture of learning, innovation, and excellence across teams
Inspire
distributed teams to achieve shared goals
Who you are
Experienced Engineer:
10+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 5 years as a Senior or Staff Engineer leading large-scale backend systems.
Technical Expert:
Deep understanding of server-side engineering principles, with hands-on experience in Java (preferred) or other backend languages.
Data-Driven:
Familiar with data systems and pipelines (SQL, Kafka, Airflow, or similar), and experienced with ensuring data quality and lineage in mission-critical flows.
Obsessed with Reliability:
Familiarity with observability and incident response practices (Prometheus, Grafana, PagerDuty, or equivalents).
Collaborative Leader:
Skilled at influencing without direct authority and aligning diverse stakeholders around shared goals.
Systems Thinker:
Balances long-term architectural vision with pragmatic short-term trade-offs to deliver business value quickly.
Mentor:
Passionate about coaching others and raising the technical bar for the organization.
Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments
Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them - voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you’re from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen.
Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application!
What’s next?
Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role.
Learn more about our hiring process here
. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.
This role is based out of our Amsterdam office. We are an office-first company and value in-person collaboration; we do not offer remote-only roles.

title: Build Tooling Engineer

We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
We’re looking for an engineer who is passionate about developer productivity, build systems, and automation to enhance and scale our internal development experience. In this role, you’ll help shape the future of our build and testing ecosystem, optimize build/test workflows, and drive automation that improves the day-to-day experience of engineers across Adyen.
What you’ll do
Work on systems and tooling that enhance developer experience by improving build and test performance, reliability, and automation at scale.
Design, maintain, and evolve the build tooling ecosystem, including, custom Linters, Static Analysis Tools, and Gradle conventions/plugins.
Optimize CI/CD pipelines and integrate intelligent tests and build selection mechanisms to reduce feedback time and increase efficiency.
Implement automation to detect and mitigate flaky tests, dependency regressions, and slow builds.

Collaborate with engineers across product domains to identify build pain points and deliver scalable solutions.

Introduce tools and best practices that enhance developer productivity and workflow consistency.
Who you are
You have experience in:
Working with build systems such as Gradle or similar, with a good understanding of build lifecycle, dependency management, and caching.
Applying test frameworks, test impact analysis, or flakiness detection techniques.
Designing or maintaining CI/CD pipelines (e.g., GitLab CI, Jenkins, Temporal, or similar).
Programming in Java, Kotlin, or Python.
Implementing developer-focused automation or tooling (e.g., internal plugins, build analyzers, or CLIs).
Working with containerized environments (Docker, Kubernetes) and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform.
Nice to have:
Experience with developer portals (e.g., Spotify Backstage).
Building build analysis or observability tooling (e.g., Gradle Profiler, Develocity Build Scans).

Understanding of monorepo architectures and dependency graph management.
You are:
Passionate about developer experience, automation, and reducing toil.
A problem solver who enjoys improving complex systems and workflows.
Curious, collaborative, and comfortable working across teams.

What’s next?
Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role.
Learn more about our hiring process here
. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.
This role is based out of our Amsterdam office. We are an office-first company and value in-person collaboration; we do not offer remote-only roles.

title: Software Engineering Manager - Issuing
description:
This is Adyen
Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.

Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
The Role
We are looking for an Engineering Manager to lead one of our core teams building out Adyen’s
Issuing Platform
. This is a high-impact, high-stakes domain that is foundational to our Financial Products offering.
In this role, you won’t just manage a team; you will own the technical direction and execution of a platform that processes millions of transactions and powers our clients' global growth. You will be responsible for scaling our card issuing capabilities and advancing our wallet offering (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay) while maintaining the highest possible standards for reliability, performance, and security.
This is a driving role for industry experts and exceptional technical leaders who thrives on complexity, demands technical excellence, and is ready to shepherd a team through a period of rapid growth.
What You’ll Do:
Drive the Roadmap:

title: Medior Machine Learning Engineer Digital((Python, MLOps, Docker, Kubernetes)
description:
.
Do you want to impact millions of users by improving digital data science products at the biggest omni-channel food retailer in the Netherlands and Belgium? Would you like to improve your technical and problem-solving skills working in a dedicated and enthusiastic team? Then we are looking for you!
As a Medior Machine Learning Engineer at Albert Heijn Technology, you will be part of our ambitious Data Science & Analytics team. You will work closely with Data Scientists, Data Analysts, Product Owners, businesses, and technology stakeholders. Together you will deliver scalable and robust Data Science products that improve the omni-channel customer journey for millions of Albert Heijn customers. For example, improving the personal bonus offers within the Mijn Bonus Box or suggesting the most suitable products during the online shopping journey using recommendation technologies.
You will use your Software Engineering skills and your interest in Analytics, Data Science and Machine Learning methods for this role. You are a flexible, versatile Software Engineer who likes to help Data Scientists and Data Analysts develop and scale software independently while keeping production and operations in mind.
Our Digital Analytics and Data Science department consists of +/- 30 enthusiastic Analysts, Data Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers with different professional backgrounds and nationalities. Our team members are passionate, curious, and pragmatic, never afraid to get their hands dirty, always learning and adapting. As a medior MLE you understand the needs within the product you work on in terms of technical improvements but also actively contribute in discussion on how to maximize business impact.
As a Medior Machine Learning Engineer at AH Technology you will:
• Design, test and build scalable machine learning solutions in a multi-disciplinary team
• Communicate your solutions clearly to a technical audience
• Translate the requirements from stakeholders into a solution and you proactively communicate about the progress of technical stories
• Contributes as part of a team with Data Scientists, Data Analysts and Product Owner
Profile:
At AH Technology, you will be working for one of the world's biggest retailers, and we know you are bursting with the ambition to keep up with us. You supplement this ambition with:
• 2+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Software Engineer, AI Engineer or Machine Learning Engineer
• Strong software development skills, able to write solid readable and testable code (Python, packaging, testing, CI/CD)
• Experience in rolling machine learning out in production (MLOps, model life cycle management, monitoring)
• Experience in maintaining and evolving an existing software project that already runs in production
• Solution design skills – you enjoy architecting and designing and scalable solutions
• Excellent communication skills, you are a team player
• Experience with big data technology(e.g. Spark), writing query's and working with large datasets
• Experience in using containers (Docker, Kubernetes) and running web servers / services /APIs in production
• You are interested in analytics, machine learning, data science and experimentation
• Based in the Netherlands
What we Offer:
At AH Technology, we aim to grow, and that is only possible if you grow with us
As we work together to build on our brand and your career, you can count on (based on 40 hours per week):
An inspiring head office in Zaandam
A competitive salary up to €70K gross per year (excl. holiday allowance)
A profit distribution on top of your salary
25,5 vacation days
A budget choice in which you can decide whether you spend it on extra days (+19,5 days), discounts or pay-outs
Flexible working hours and the possibility to work from home
An excellent pension plan
Courses from our Albert Heijn TOP Academy
Attractive discounts on various insurance policies
A travel allowance for traveling to and from Zaandam/ Business OV-chip card
A MacBook and iPhone
10% discount on your grocery shopping in our Albert Heijn stores, up to € 300 per year
We find it important that you can be yourself at Albert Heijn in an environment that is open to different perspectives, an environment where we are curious and appreciate each other.
We’re Albert Heijn, food retail reinventors 🚀
Albert Heijn goes way back. More than 135 years, to be exact. So, for millions of people, food shopping is synonymous with the Albert Heijn brand. We didn’t achieve this with a complacent, ‘hey, if it works today, it’ll work tomorrow’ approach, but by continually rethinking our propositions and staying plugged in to customer demands. More than 125,000 colleagues? Check. Over 1,250 stores in the Netherlands and Flanders? Check. The leading online food retail platform? Check, we build, run and love it.
Together we make eating better the easy choice. For everyone.
The world around us has changed, and significantly at that. These changes have an impact on the lives of our customers, colleagues and partners. And therefore, also on Albert Heijn. We’re moving towards a new world with new opportunities and challenges. Albert Heijn wants more people to eat fresher, healthier and more sustainable food. Eating brings us together, keeps us healthier and contributes positively to the world. Our mission is: Together we make eating better the easy choice. For everyone.
Albert Heijn the Netherlands is B Corp certified. This underscores our purpose, through which we aim to contribute to a healthier, more social, and sustainable society. B Corp certification is a designation that a business meets high standards of verified performance, accountability and transparency.
Ready to contribute to our mission?
Do you want to play an active role in changing the food retail landscape as we know it? Then hop on board and join us on our expedition. Would you like to learn more first, before you apply to Albert Heijn? We’re happy to tell you everything about this vacancy! An assessment might be part of our selection process.
Apply
Do you want to play an active role in changing the food retail landscape as we know it? Then hop on board and join us on our expedition. Apply via the button on this page. We ask you not to send a standard motivation letter, but to formulate an answer to the following questions:
Why do you think you will be successful in this position and what kind of technical skills do you bring with you for this role?
What is one of your personal strengths and one skill you wish to improve or develop?
Want to know more before you apply for our role at AH Technology? Peter Dirks, Talent Acquisition Partner Data Science & Analytics, is happy to tell you everything about this vacancy via
peter.dirks@ah.nl
.

Master’s, PhD, or equivalent experience in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Physics, etc.). Strong advantage for candidates whose MSc or PhD thesis work is related to NLP, showcasing deep research capabilities in this field. End-to-End R&D Proficiency: Demonstrated experience in designing and executing end-to-end research and development plans, from problem definition and data preparation to model deployment and performance monitoring. Proven Impact: A track record of delivering measurable business value through the development and implementation of large-scale machine learning models. Experience on multiple machine learning facets: working with large data sets, model development, statistics, experimentation, data visualization, optimization, software development. Experience collaborating cross functionally in the development of machine learning products (e.g. Developers, UX specialists, Product Managers, etc.). Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal. Leading by example, gaining respect through actions, not your title. Developing your team and motivating them to achieve their goals.
